process_timeline.action: Read README.md, README.en.md, installing_guide.md, contributing.md, pyproject.toml, setup.py, requirements files, build scripts, test scripts
Started Docker container with Ubuntu 24.04, mounted repo at /workspace
Installed base build tools: python3, pip, gcc, g++, make, setuptools, wheel
Installed torch (CPU), pytest, coverage, einops, numpy, safetensors for UT execution
Ran unit tests via python3 UT/run.py
Ran unit tests via pytest UT/ -v
Skipped - all examples require torch_npu and NPU hardware

build.failure_reason: Build requires Ascend CANN toolkit (ASCEND_TOOLKIT_HOME) which is only available on systems with Ascend NPU hardware. The build_ops.sh script requires msopgen tool from CANN, and build_plugin.sh requires torch_npu and CANN setenv.bash. Neither can be satisfied on a standard x86_64 Ubuntu system.
build.details: The setup.py CustomBuildPy first runs build_ops.sh (Ascend C operators), then build_plugin.sh (PyTorch plugins). Both fail without CANN toolkit.

failures.root_cause: pytest does not apply the torch_npu mock defined in UT/run.py. The mock is set up in run.py before module imports, but pytest collects and runs tests directly without going through run.py.

results.name: examples/wan/infer_t2v.sh
results.reason: Requires torch_npu, model weights, and Ascend NPU hardware

results.name: examples/service/server.py
results.reason: Requires torch_npu and model inference setup

documentation_gaps.description: The documentation does not describe how to build the package without the Ascend CANN toolkit. The installing_guide.md only covers container and bare-metal installation on systems with Ascend NPU hardware.
The test scripts (run_test.sh, run_UT_test.sh) have --cpu_only flags but the documentation does not explain what subset of tests can run on CPU or how to set up the environment for CPU-only testing.

The UT/run.py file mocks torch_npu at module level, but this mock is not applied when running tests via pytest directly. The documentation does not mention this discrepancy or how to run pytest with the mock.
The requirements.txt lists many packages (diffusers, transformers, open_clip_torch, etc.) but the installing guide does not explain when each is needed or how they relate to different models.
contributing.md only contains links to Ascend community guidelines but does not provide project-specific build/test instructions for contributors.

problems_encountered.problem: Build failed: 'Can not find toolkit path, please set ASCEND_TOOLKIT_HOME'
problems_encountered.solution_attempted: Searched build/build_ops.sh for fallback paths - only checks ASCEND_TOOLKIT_HOME env var, /usr/local/Ascend/ascend-toolkit/latest, and /home/<user>/Ascend/ascend-toolkit/latest. None available on standard Ubuntu.
problems_encountered.root_cause: MindIE-SD is designed for Ascend NPU hardware. The CANN toolkit (Ascend Computing Architecture) is required for building custom operators and plugins. The toolkit is only available on systems with Ascend hardware.

problems_encountered.problem: UT run.py failed with 'No module named einops'
problems_encountered.solution_attempted: Installed einops and numpy via pip

problems_encountered.problem: UT run.py failed with 'No module named safetensors'
pytest fails with 'No module named torch_npu' for 17 tests
problems_encountered.error_output: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'torch_npu' in mindiesd/layers/flash_attn/prompt_flash_attn.py line 14
problems_encountered.solution_attempted: The torch_npu mock in UT/run.py is not applied by pytest. This is a test infrastructure issue - pytest needs conftest.py or pytest fixtures to mock torch_npu.
problems_encountered.root_cause: The mock is defined in UT/run.py which is only used by the unittest runner. pytest collects tests directly without going through run.py. A conftest.py with the same mock would fix this.
